behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is mainly module handoff (branch-diff data + age threshold + PR-presence state), not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: no behavior text requires real HTTP, subprocess, or OS events. REJECTION-GATE: higher System was considered, but rejected because the behavior text only says to identify state predicates ("commits not in main", "older than threshold", "no open PR") and does not require live infrastructure or external round-trip visibility.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real detector orchestration with fixture branch metadata and fixture PR-state provider; assert stale=true only when all three predicates are true, and stale=false when any predicate is false.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when bootstrap wiring, detector output, and warning formatter/sink interact. REAL-INFRA: behavior does not require real OS/network/subprocess, only that startup path emits warning content. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered, but rejected because the behavior text requires emission of warning context, which can be validated through in-process startup wiring and captured output sink without external boundary readback.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start initialization with a stale branch state injected through the normal provider chain; assert a warning event/message is emitted once and includes actionable branch context fields.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: none; this is explicitly one local deterministic mapping function. REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP: none apply.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven test on the classifier function only: given computed branch states, assert exact label outputs `clean` or `stale-no-pr` with deterministic results.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA and SYSTEM TRIAD both trigger: behavior explicitly says "against live repo and PR state" (real external state/contract) and requires rendered externally visible output classification. Mocking would hide real repo/API shape mismatches and stale-state readback errors.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` in a real repo context with real PR-state source; verify rendered output round-trip shows `clean` for non-stale branch and `STALE` for branch with commits and no PR.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is cross-module logic (PR-state interpretation + stale classifier gating), beyond a single function. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess is stated, no AI dependency, no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered, but rejected because behavior text targets classification correctness for states "open or merged PRs" and does not require live API contract verification in this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run detector/classifier pipeline with representative branch states linked to PR states `open` and `merged`; assert output is never `stale-no-pr` for those cases.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: correctness depends on true git-history semantics under rebase/squash (commit-hash rewrites), typically exercised via real git operations/subprocess and repository state transitions. Mocking commit graphs can miss real ancestry and merge-base edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: In a real test repo, create branch commits, perform rebase and squash-merge scenarios that rewrite hashes, then run staleness detection and verify the branch is not classified as unPRed stale afterward.
